name: python-testing-patterns
description: Implement comprehensive testing strategies with pytest, fixtures, mocking, and test-driven development. Use when writing Python tests, setting up test suites, or implementing testing best practices.
capabilities: Create pytest test files with proper structure using AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert)
Write fixtures with different scopes (function, class, session, module) for setup and teardown
Mock external dependencies using unittest.mock patch decorators and context managers
Use pytest.mark.parametrize for testing multiple input combinations efficiently
Implement test-driven development workflows with red-green-refactor cycles
Set up integration tests for APIs and databases with proper isolation
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 87 static findings are false positives. The detected patterns (external_commands, network, env_access) are documentation examples of pytest commands, HTTP mocking patterns, and test fixture configurations - all legitimate testing education content. No actual security threats present.
valueStatement: Writing maintainable tests is challenging. This skill provides comprehensive pytest patterns including fixtures, mocking, parameterization, and TDD workflows to help you build reliable test suites.
limitations: Does not cover specific framework testing (Django, Flask, FastAPI specific test tools)
No coverage of performance testing or load testing tools
Does not include security testing or penetration testing guidance
Limited coverage of GUI testing or web automation (selenium/playwright)

useCases.targetUser: Developers starting new Python projects who need testing infrastructure
useCases.title: New Python Project Testing Setup
useCases.description: Set up a complete test suite for a new Python project with fixtures, conftest.py configuration, and CI/CD integration

useCases.targetUser: Engineers maintaining legacy Python codebases without tests
useCases.title: Legacy Code Test Coverage
useCases.description: Add tests to existing untested code using mocking to isolate units and parameterization to cover edge cases

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers testing API integrations and service interactions
useCases.title: API Integration Testing
useCases.description: Write integration tests for REST APIs using fixtures for test data and mocking for external service calls

promptTemplates.title: Basic Test Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create pytest tests for a function called [FUNCTION_NAME] that [DESCRIPTION]. Use the AAA pattern.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to write unit tests for a simple function

promptTemplates.title: Fixture Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create pytest fixtures with [SCOPE] scope to provide [RESOURCE] for tests. Include proper setup and teardown.
promptTemplates.scenario: When tests need shared resources like database connections or API clients

promptTemplates.title: Mocking External Calls
promptTemplates.prompt: Write tests that mock [EXTERNAL_SERVICE] calls using unittest.mock. Show both patch decorator and context manager approaches.
promptTemplates.scenario: When testing code that makes HTTP requests or calls external APIs

promptTemplates.title: Parameterized Testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Create parameterized tests for [FUNCTION] using pytest.mark.parametrize to test [EDGE_CASES].
promptTemplates.scenario: When testing a function with multiple input combinations or edge cases

bestPractices: Use descriptive test names that explain what is being tested and expected behavior
Keep tests independent with proper setup/teardown - no shared state between tests
Follow AAA pattern: Arrange (setup), Act (execute), Assert (verify) clearly separated
antiPatterns: Avoid test names like test_1 or test_function that do not describe the test purpose
Do not mix multiple assertions in one test when they could be separate tests
Avoid testing implementation details instead of behavior - test what the code does, not how

faq.question: What is the difference between unit tests and integration tests?
faq.answer: Unit tests test individual functions or classes in isolation. Integration tests test how multiple components work together, such as testing API endpoints with a real database.

faq.question: How do I mock a function that does not use requests library?
faq.answer: Use unittest.mock.patch decorator or context manager to replace any function or class with a Mock object. Import the function path as a string: @patch('module.function_name').

faq.question: What is fixture scope and when should I use each?
faq.answer: Function scope (default) creates fixture for each test. Class scope once per test class. Module scope once per module. Session scope once per entire test session. Use session/module for expensive resources.

faq.question: How do I test exceptions with pytest?
faq.answer: Use pytest.raises() as a context manager: with pytest.raises(ExpectedException): your_function(). Optionally pass match parameter to check exception message.

faq.question: What is test-driven development (TDD)?
faq.answer: TDD is a development approach where you write tests before code. Cycle: Write failing test (red), write minimal code to pass (green), refactor code (refactor). Helps ensure testable design.

faq.question: How do I run only tests matching a keyword?
faq.answer: Use pytest -k flag: pytest -k 'test_name_pattern' runs only tests with names matching the pattern. Use pytest -m marker to run tests with specific markers.
